title: T-061 — Password policy enforcement library (NIST: min-12, HIBP, last-5 history)
prompt:
# T-061 — Password policy enforcement library

PRD: `docs/prd/00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md` — sections: §13 (F-U5 NIST password policy)
Project root: `/home/user/monopilot-kira`
Audit source: `_meta/audits/2026-05-07-foundation-wave-a-prd-drift-audit.md` Critical drift #3
Severity: P1 (closes coverage gap; consumed by T-011 Supabase Auth wiring)

## Goal
Create a NIST-compliant password policy library at `packages/auth/src/password-policy.ts` that enforces minimum 12 characters, checks against the Have I Been Pwned (HIBP) k-anonymity API, and maintains a `password_history` table with last-5 history blocking. Used by T-011 sign-up/change-password flows.

## Implementation contract
Create or extend `packages/auth/` (Drizzle schema + service module). The package may need to be scaffolded if it does not exist yet (`packages/auth/package.json`, `tsconfig.json`).

Policy module `packages/auth/src/password-policy.ts`:
- `export type PasswordPolicyError = 'too_short' | 'pwned' | 'reused' | 'too_common'`
- `export async function validateNewPassword(opts: { userId: string; orgId: string; newPassword: string; pool: Pool }): Promise<{ ok: true } | { ok: false; reasons: PasswordPolicyError[] }>`
- Internal: minimum-length check (12 chars).
- Internal: HIBP k-anonymity check — SHA1 the password, send first 5 hex chars to `https://api.pwnedpasswords.com/range/{prefix}`, scan response for the remaining 35 chars; do NOT send the full hash or password. Honor a `process.env.HIBP_DISABLED=true` env-flag for offline test runs.
- Internal: last-5 history check — `select password_hash from password_history where user_id=$1 order by created_at desc limit 5`; argon2-verify the new password against each.
- On success, the caller (T-011) inserts the new argon2id hash into `password_history`; this library exposes `recordPasswordHistory(userId, hash, pool)` helper.

Migration `packages/db/migrations/018-password-history.sql`:
- `password_history(user_id uuid not null references users(id) on delete cascade, password_hash text not null, created_at timestamptz not null default now(), primary key (user_id, created_at))`
- ENABLE + FORCE RLS with policy `user_id IN (select id from users where org_id = app.current_org_id())`.
- Index on `(user_id, created_at desc)` for the last-5 lookup.
- Optional: trigger that automatically deletes rows beyond the most recent 5 per user.

Unit tests `packages/auth/src/__tests__/password-policy.test.ts`:
- Known-bad vectors: `'password'`, `'12345678'`, `'qwertyuiop'`, `'short'` (8 chars), `'Test1234'` (11 chars) — each must fail with the appropriate reason.
- Known-good vector: `'TG7v$Bk2#xQ8nMz3'` (16 chars, randomly generated) — must pass HIBP (mock or HIBP_DISABLED).
- Reuse vector: pre-seed `password_history` with argon2 hash of `'OldPwd1234567'`, then attempt to set the same password — must fail with `'reused'`.
- HIBP mock: stub `fetch` to return a known-pwned password range response (e.g., the well-known SHA1 prefix for `'password'`).
- Min-length boundary test: 11 chars fails, 12 chars passes (other gates aside).

## Files (scope)
- packages/auth/package.json [create-or-modify]
- packages/auth/tsconfig.json [create-or-modify]
- packages/auth/src/password-policy.ts [create]
- packages/auth/src/__tests__/password-policy.test.ts [create]
- packages/auth/src/index.ts [create-or-modify — export validateNewPassword + recordPasswordHistory]
- packages/auth/eslint.config.mjs [create — extend tooling/eslint/base.mjs from T-055]
- packages/db/migrations/018-password-history.sql [create]
- packages/db/schema/password-history.ts [create — Drizzle definition]
- packages/db/schema/index.ts [modify — re-export]
- packages/db/__tests__/password-history-rls.integration.test.ts [create]
- _meta/atomic-tasks/00-foundation/STATUS.md [modify — migration ordering lock]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given a password shorter than 12 characters, when `validateNewPassword` runs, then it returns `{ ok: false, reasons: ['too_short'] }`.
2. Given a known-pwned password (e.g., `'password'` whose SHA1 prefix is `5BAA6` and known to be in HIBP), when `validateNewPassword` runs with HIBP enabled (or with the mock returning a hit), then it returns `{ ok: false, reasons: ['pwned'] }`.
3. Given a password identical to one of the user's last 5 history entries, when `validateNewPassword` runs, then it returns `{ ok: false, reasons: ['reused'] }`.
4. Given a strong, non-pwned, non-reused password, when `validateNewPassword` runs, then it returns `{ ok: true }`.
5. Given migration 018, when the runner applies it, then `password_history` exists with RLS enabled, the FK to users, and the index on (user_id, created_at desc).
6. Given the RLS test, when `app_user` connects under one org context, then SELECT on password_history returns only that org's user rows.
7. Given the unit-test vector pack, when `pnpm --filter @monopilot/auth test` runs, then all 5+ known-bad vectors fail with the expected reason and 1 known-good vector passes.

## Test strategy
- RED: write `packages/auth/src/__tests__/password-policy.test.ts` with the full known-bad/known-good vector pack BEFORE implementing the module — ensure each test fails for the right reason.
- Mock `fetch` for HIBP via `vi.fn()` returning canned k-anonymity responses.
- Use `process.env.HIBP_DISABLED=true` to disable the live HIBP call in CI when the mock is not in place.
- Pin SQLSTATE for the RLS integration test (`42501` for cross-org SELECT denial).
- Run `pnpm --filter @monopilot/auth test` and `pnpm --filter @monopilot/db test`.

## Risk red lines
- Do NOT send the full password or full SHA1 hash to HIBP; only the first 5 hex chars (k-anonymity).
- Do NOT store passwords in plaintext anywhere — `password_history.password_hash` is argon2id only.
- Do NOT use bcrypt for new password hashes (use argon2id per PRD §13).
- Do NOT block the auth flow on HIBP latency; add a 2s timeout and fail-open to a soft warning (or hard-fail per env flag).
- Do NOT skip the RLS migration; password history is highly sensitive.
- Do NOT use bare `.rejects.toThrow()` in DB tests — pin SQLSTATE.
- Do NOT use raw `new pg.Pool` (per T-055/T-058 lint rule).

## Closeout evidence
Provide: changed_files, test_commands_and_results, acceptance_criteria_status (per AC), deviations_from_prd, git_status. Use the test-driven-development skill (RED known-bad vector pack before implementation) and requesting-code-review at GREEN.

Do not introduce new ESLint rules (T-055 owns).
